proposed_from.topic: Coupling dichotomy and sharp non-rectangular identified set for offline contextual-bandit policy value V(pi) under JOINT misspecification: a propensity Marginal-Sensitivity-Model budget Gamma together with a structural epsilon-tube (fixed center law F0, fixed metric, stated support regularity) on the conditional potential-outcome law that the MSM clips. Prove the dichotomy: an additive tube on the conditional-outcome MEAN decouples (joint set = rectangular product, trivial), while a tube on the conditional QUANTILE function couples with the active MSM clip (joint set strictly inside the rectangular product). Deliver the closed-form sharp coupled endpoint, the non-rectangularity gap Delta_rect(Gamma,epsilon)>=0 and its positive region, and a Neyman-orthogonal efficient one-step EIF estimator with uniform inference that corrects the rectangular widening of the Frauen et al. (arXiv:2502.13022, ICLR 2026) sharp off-policy estimator V-hat^{+,*}; separate endpoint sharpness from EIF regularity away from the coupling-threshold kink.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: The closest published comparator is HessFrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2026: it identifies and efficiently estimates the sharp MSM off-policy value endpoint, but it does not impose a fixed-center structural tube on the same latent conditional potential-outcome law. The next closest published comparator is ZhangXuTan2025: it intersects treatment and outcome sensitivity restrictions, but for expected potential outcomes under an outcome-mean restriction, not for offline policy value under a conditional-quantile-process tube. DornGuo2023 supplies the active rank-clipping geometry, but not the joint policy-value endpoint. The precise gap is therefore a law-level compatibility problem: marginally sharp MSM and tube envelopes need not be jointly attainable by one latent law. The core file targets exactly that gap with a closed-form coupled endpoint, a strict non-rectangularity region against Frauen-style rectangular widening, and a separate conjectural efficiency layer away from the clip-tube kink.

seed_details.kernel: Sharp upper/lower offline-policy endpoints under the intersection of an MSM budget and a fixed-center conditional-quantile tube, with closed-form clipped-and-capped quantile representation and a nonnegative rectangularity gap.
seed_details.red_team: Immediate from HessFrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2026 plus a pointwise min cap, because the MSM extremizer and the tube can be optimized separately.
seed_details.rebuttal: The same latent arm law must satisfy both restrictions; separable marginal extremizers need not be jointly feasible, so the coupled endpoint is not the rectangular product.
seed_details.closest_gap: Hess et al. solve MSM-only policy-value bounds; Zhang-Xu-Tan intersect treatment and outcome sensitivity for mean targets, not fixed-center quantile-law policy-value endpoints.

seed_details.kernel: Matched theorem pair: additive conditional-mean tubes decouple and remain rectangular, while conditional-quantile tubes couple with MSM rank clipping and produce a strict non-rectangular joint set on a nondegenerate region.
seed_details.red_team: Just a pedagogical contrast; the mean case is trivial and the quantile case is a restatement of process compatibility from the rejected QTE angle.
seed_details.rebuttal: The contribution is the policy-value-level if-and-only-if contrast tied to a named sharp OPE workflow, not a free-standing compatibility anecdote.
seed_details.closest_gap: Yadlowsky et al. explain scalar mean sensitivity; Dorn-Guo explain rank clipping; no paper states the offline-policy mean-vs-quantile joint-misspecification dichotomy.

seed_details.kernel: Partition the coupled endpoint into regular interior, atom/flat-CDF, and support-boundary regimes; state which parts of sharpness and strict-gap logic survive in each regime.
seed_details.red_team: Routine technical appendix work once the endpoint is known.
seed_details.rebuttal: Here the edge cases are load-bearing because the gap can vanish exactly at boundary contact and the inference object changes there.
seed_details.closest_gap: Firpo gives regular quantile differentiability; Fan-Park treat partial-ID quantile inversion; neither resolves the joint MSM-plus-quantile-tube endpoint atlas.

seed_details.kernel: Conjectural semiparametric efficiency split: away from the coupling kink, a corrected one-step estimator has an EIF equal to the Hess/Frauen score minus a gate-adjusted excess term; at the kink only directional or conservative inference is regular.
seed_details.red_team: Too derivative of Hess/Frauen once the new endpoint is written down.
seed_details.rebuttal: Pathwise differentiability is exactly what fails at the clip-tube threshold, so the inference frontier is not inherited mechanically from the MSM-only paper.
seed_details.closest_gap: Hess et al. derive the MSM-only EIF; Chernozhukov et al. give orthogonality templates; no paper handles the nonsmooth joint endpoint map created by quantile-tube clipping.

seed_details.kernel: Construct a binary or finite-grid class of observed laws on which the coupled set is strictly inside the rectangular widening over a positive-measure region of policy weights and tube radii.
seed_details.red_team: Single-witness pathology dressed up as a theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed only works if upgraded to a region-of-laws statement; standing alone it is too weak.
seed_details.closest_gap: The v1 bank asked for a concrete witness, but witness-only content is below the bar unless tied to a generic region theorem.

seed_details.kernel: Characterize the joint identified set by a support-function dual or tail-integral envelope rather than by separate upper/lower formulas.
seed_details.red_team: Representation-only partial-ID work without new estimator or regime content.
seed_details.rebuttal: Useful as a derivation device, but too thin as the main kernel unless paired with the endpoint correction and inference rung.
seed_details.closest_gap: No exact dual is published for this joint policy-value problem, but the representation alone does not clear the novelty bar.

seed_details.kernel: Extend the coupled endpoint from policy value to policy improvement relative to a baseline policy, preserving the clip-tube interaction in regret space.
seed_details.red_team: Routine corollary once value bounds are known.
seed_details.rebuttal: The improvement target is policy-facing, but it is downstream of the harder value theorem and not the right first kernel.
seed_details.closest_gap: Hess et al. already extend MSM-only theory to policy improvement; the new content would still depend on the unresolved joint endpoint.

seed_details.kernel: Define a critical tube radius epsilon*(Gamma,pi) at which the rectangularity gap closes, and characterize sign/strictness regions by active tail headroom and support contact.
seed_details.red_team: Just algebra on top of the coupled endpoint once that theorem is proved.
seed_details.rebuttal: As a standalone paper it is secondary, but as a proposition inside the main angle it sharpens the policy audit message materially.
seed_details.closest_gap: No paper defines a critical structural-tube threshold for when a published sharp MSM OPE workflow can be widened exactly.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:joint-feasible / thm:quantile-coupled-endpoint
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The coupled sharp endpoint is conditional on nonempty intersection of the MSM weight window and the fixed-center quantile tube on policy-relevant arms.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The identification theorem is a sharp bound for the restricted nonempty joint class; incompatible laws yield an empty class, not a finite corrected endpoint.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:regular-contact / conj:eif-one-step
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The EIF and root-n inference claim assumes a unique upper optimizer and stable finite-union contact face rather than deriving optimizer regularity.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The inference conjecture is only for regular-contact laws; optimizer switching and support-edge contact remain directional or conservative-inference regimes.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: conj:eif-one-step / fixed center law
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Q0_a and epsilon are treated as fixed analyst inputs rather than estimated nuisance functions.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The one-step claim is conditional on a known center law and known tube radius; external estimation of the center law would require a different target or an added influence contribution.

banked_reason: Identification core sound and field-novel (decouple/couple dichotomy: thm:mean-decoupling rectangular mean-tube vs thm:quantile-coupled-endpoint sharp coupled quantile-tube endpoint; prop:rect-gap non-rectangularity gap Delta_rect>=0 with exact iff-zero), and the EIF decomposition phi_cpl=phi_MSM-phi_tube with contact-condition Neyman-orthogonality (centered contact score mean-zero under the latent optimizing law) DERIVED; but D0.5 twice escalated because the semiparametric inference rung's plug-in consistency (uniform feasible-class / argmax convergence) and the substrate-gate registration of the baseline MSM EIF remain genuinely undischarged under the strict typed D0 gate.
